title: AiNarration
description: The forced structured output of Golavo's optional AI layer, produced ONLY from a MatchEvidenceBundle. It carries no probabilities and no authority: it explains and cites the engine's numbers. Every number in any text field must resolve to an allowed_numbers id in the source bundle, or the whole narration is rejected and the app falls back to local-only. The OPTIONAL background lane is a separate, clearly-badged channel of qualitative colour from the model's own general knowledge — forbidden from stating any number and never folded into evidence. This is the wire schema the model is constrained to; the served narration additionally stamps provenance (see AiNarrationEnvelope in golavo_server.ai_gateway).

claims.description: Grounded observations about this forecast. Each must cite at least one source_id; claims without one are dropped.

scenarios.description: Qualitative 'what could happen' sketches. Same numeric and citation rules as claims.

candidate_facts.description: Proposed NEW facts the model believes are relevant but that are NOT in the bundle. They are never rendered as engine facts and never touch a probability; each needs an exact source quote and, downstream, allowlisted-source + quote-match + explicit user confirmation before it could ever become a typed feature.

background.description: OPTIONAL. Qualitative context from the model's OWN general knowledge (managers, playing-style reputations, rivalry/tournament history) — enabled only when the user opts in. Clearly badged as not-Golavo-data and may be outdated. Forbidden from stating any number in any form; numberless notes only. Never folded into evidence, notebooks, or allowed_numbers. Not in `required` so older outputs stay valid.

number_refs.description: The allowed_numbers ids for every number used in `text`.
